name: Hand Pose & Gesture Detection
promptBlock:
Hand Pose: Use VNDetectHumanHandPoseRequest with VNImageRequestHandler for single-frame gesture detection. Always import Vision and AVFoundation. Use AVCaptureVideoDataOutput to receive CMSampleBuffer frames continuously, but only analyze a frame when the user triggers a capture (e.g. taps a button or a countdown ends).

CRITICAL — VNImageRequestHandler orientation: ALWAYS use .right for BOTH front and back camera. Do NOT use .leftMirrored for the front camera — it inverts the Y-axis relationship between fingertips and knuckles, causing Paper to be classified as Rock. Use .right for both cameras.

// REQUIRES PLIST: NSCameraUsageDescription

All joint names come from VNHumanHandPoseObservation.JointName. Always check confidence >= 0.7. A finger is extended when tipY > mcpY (Y increases upward in Vision's normalized space when using .right orientation). Test all gestures on both front and rear camera — Y-axis orientation behaves differently depending on how the user holds their hand relative to each camera.
antiPatterns: Using .leftMirrored orientation for front camera — it inverts the Y-axis relationship between fingertips and knuckles, causing extended fingers to have LOWER tip Y than their MCPs. This makes isExtended() always return false and every gesture classify as Rock. Use .right for both front and back camera.
Using VNDetectHumanBodyPoseRequest instead of VNDetectHumanHandPoseRequest for finger/hand detection
Using joint names from VNHumanBodyPoseObservation.JointName (e.g. .leftShoulder) — hand pose requires VNHumanHandPoseObservation.JointName
Running VNImageRequestHandler on the main thread
Using confidence threshold below 0.7 — low-confidence points produce unreliable tip/knuckle comparisons
Running hand pose detection on every camera frame — capture one frame on user trigger instead
Silently ignoring no-hand-detected case — always update a statusMessage the UI displays
Omitting the camera flip button when AVFoundation camera is used — always include a camera.rotate.fill SF Symbol button in the top corner that calls cameraManager.flipCamera()

When using the rear camera, the Y-axis in Vision's coordinate space may behave differently than expected. If paper (open hand) is not being detected on the rear camera, the isExtended() comparison may need to account for the physical orientation of the hand relative to the rear camera. Always test gesture detection on both front and rear camera before considering the app functional.

commonErrors: Paper classified as Rock — caused by .leftMirrored orientation inverting Y-axis; fix by using .right orientation for both cameras
